title: Happy Place
author: Emily Henry
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Happy Place" book by Emily Henry?
Options:
A. You're very smart, as my dad would say. I was an athlete in college to get into medical school, but I'm actually in law school, not med school. This is Wyn, my fiancé, who works at a bookstore and does furniture repair. No worries that you don't recall him.  
B. You have a lot of intelligence, as my father would tell me. I played sports in university to get into med school, but I'm really in law school, not medical studies. Meet Wyn, my future husband, who has jobs at a bookshop and repairing furniture. Don't stress that you don't know anything about the love of my life.
C. You're clearly very bright, as my dad would point out. I was a student athlete to try to get into med school, but actually I'm in law school, not medical school. This is Wyn, my fiance who works at a bookstore and repairs furniture. No big deal that you don't remember my soulmate at all.
D. Youre clearly very intelligent. He is, I say, right as he says, I was a student athlete. Well, to get into medical school, anyway, Dad says. I full-body wince, but Wyn squeezes my knee reassuringly. Im actually not a med student, he says. Hes in law school, Phil, Mom says, irritable. Thats Sabrina and Parth, I say. Wyn works at the bookstore and does furniture repair. You know, I think, the one Im engaged to. But I think it with a smile that hopefully says, No big deal that you dont remember the slightest thing about the love of my life.
Answer:

options.A: You're very smart, as my dad would say. I was an athlete in college to get into medical school, but I'm actually in law school, not med school. This is Wyn, my fiancé, who works at a bookstore and does furniture repair. No worries that you don't recall him.
options.B: You have a lot of intelligence, as my father would tell me. I played sports in university to get into med school, but I'm really in law school, not medical studies. Meet Wyn, my future husband, who has jobs at a bookshop and repairing furniture. Don't stress that you don't know anything about the love of my life.
options.C: You're clearly very bright, as my dad would point out. I was a student athlete to try to get into med school, but actually I'm in law school, not medical school. This is Wyn, my fiance who works at a bookstore and repairs furniture. No big deal that you don't remember my soulmate at all.
options.D: Youre clearly very intelligent. He is, I say, right as he says, I was a student athlete. Well, to get into medical school, anyway, Dad says. I full-body wince, but Wyn squeezes my knee reassuringly. Im actually not a med student, he says. Hes in law school, Phil, Mom says, irritable. Thats Sabrina and Parth, I say. Wyn works at the bookstore and does furniture repair. You know, I think, the one Im engaged to. But I think it with a smile that hopefully says, No big deal that you dont remember the slightest thing about the love of my life.
